details.text: Derivative: أَثْقَال is the plural of ثِقْلٌ and refers to a heavy load or burden which is carried whilst حِمْلٌ and its plural أَحْمال refer to a lesser load or burden which is carried as compared to ثِقْلٌ.
Derivative: أَوْثَانًا is the plural of وَثَنٌ and refers to an idol that remains in its place for a long time and also an uncarved idol.
Derivative: الْبَلَاغُ refers to the clear communication and conveyance. الْبَلَاغُ is the same as التَّبْلِيغ from بَلَّغَ which means to clarify and communicate or convey (i.e., the message).
Derivative: يُبْدِئُ from أَبْدَأَ which is the hyperbolised form of بَدَأَ which means to begin referring to the very beginning or initial step, initiation or cause of something etc.

Derivative: يُفْتَنُونَ is from فَتَنَ and it means to burn gold until its impurities are removed i.e., the testing of gold to make sure it is purified and that is فِتْنَة. The general meaning is to be tested and put to trial.
Derivative: يُنشِئُ from أَنْشَأَ means to bring a new life or to make something rise from anew.
Derivative: تُقْلَبُونَ – أَقْلَبَ means to make something turn around and تُقْلَبُ means to be turned around.
Derivative: يَئِسُوا from يَئِسَ which means to lose hope, for hope to die out and be in in a state of despair.

Derivative: مَّوَدَّةَ is a strong bond of love and it also refers to wanting or desiring to acquire something for yourself. In this verse the meaning is: “You have only taken, other than Allah, idols as a bond of affection (مَّوَدَّةَ) among yourselves in the worldly life”.
Derivative: نَادِي refers to a place where people gather and talk with each other. Is used to refer to clubs and also public places where people gather together.
Derivative: الْغَابِرِينَ from غَبَرَ which means to be left behind and unable to catch up anymore.

- ضَاقَ بِ something ذَرْعًا means not be able to stand or bear something, in the case where ضَاقَ refers to being constrained and ذَرْعًا refers to power or ability i.e., not having the strength or ability to deal with something- it is too much or unbearable.
Derivative: رِجْزٌ refers to a calamity and punishment from Allah.
Derivative: تَعْثَوْا from عَثَا – يَعْثُو which means to go around causing corruption and mischief i.e., in the land.
Derivative: جَاثِمِينَ refers to their state of just being bodies lying around and prone where they had fallen to their deaths following the punishment that had wiped them out.
Derivative: الرَّجْفَةُ refers to a violent shaking i.e., like an earthquake.

Derivative: مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ means people with insight and perceptiveness. Here it can mean that they viewed themselves to be people of great insight and intelligence or that they did possess that, but it was not applied in the most important of matters i.e., in relation to belief and acting righteously.
Derivative: سَابِقِينَ refers to those who get ahead and outrun i.e., escape.
Derivative: يَسْبِقُونَا from سَيَقَ – يَسْبِقُ to get ahead and escape.
Derivative: أَوْهَنَ means the weakest and frailest. In Arabic they say that وَهَنٌ refers to weakness in action and character.
- بِالْحَقِّ means by the truth, truly or with purpose.
- أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ “Establish the prayer” differs from “Pray”. إِقامَة (Establishing) implies making something stand up and become strong – establishing it foundations and building blocks.
Derivative: يَجْحَدُ from جَحَدَ means to deny, reject or refuse to acknowledge something despite knowing that it is true.
Derivative: يَأْتِي from أَتَى means to come and arrive but also to attack.
Derivative: بَغْتَةً means: “From out of nowhere” i.e., suddenly and in surprise.
Derivative: يَغْشَى from غَشِيَ means to be covered by something where the thing being covered can barely be seen.
Derivative: غُرَفًا refers to rooms with high ceilings.

Derivative: جَاهَدَ – يُجَاهِدُ – جِهاد / مُجَاهَدَةً is usually understood to refer to fighting and physical struggle. However, in Makkah to struggle and persevere in the face of the tests, including torture, without fighting back, is also being referred to as being Jihad.

Derivative: الْحَيَوَانُ is the Mubaalaghah (exaggeration) form of حَياة (life). Some scholars have stated that this is used because there is no sleep in the hereafter which differs from the حَياة (life) of this life i.e., it is a life with more life in it.
Derivative: مَثْوًى is a place that you reside and stay in for a very long time; generation upon generation.

Derivative: سُبُل means pathway and is the plural of سَبيل. سَبيل is like the smaller road that leads to the صِراط which is the main road. Islam is the الصِّراطُ المُسْتَقِيم the main road that leads to Allah and paradise. سُبُل are the pathways that lead to the صِراط i.e., to Allah, His pleasure and rewards.
